intro: The DASH diet (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) is a scientifically proven eating plan to help lower blood pressure. This calculator helps you determine your daily sodium target based on the standard or strict DASH approach. Values are current for 2026 and regularly updated to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **daily sodium in mg**. Choose your DASH plan level (standard or strict) and calculate your personalized sodium target instantly.

explanation:
## Understanding DASH and Daily Sodium

The DASH diet (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) is an eating plan developed by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to help lower blood pressure naturally. Research shows the DASH diet can reduce systolic blood pressure by 5-10 mmHg within two weeks when followed consistently.

## How This Calculator Works

This calculator helps you determine your daily sodium maximum based on your chosen DASH plan:

- **Standard DASH**: 2,300 mg of sodium per day
- **Strict DASH**: 1,500 mg of sodium per day

It then automatically converts your sodium limit to its salt equivalent. Remember: 1 gram of salt contains approximately 400 mg of sodium.

## Sodium and Your Health

High sodium intake is linked to elevated blood pressure. By limiting sodium as DASH recommends, you support heart health and help manage hypertension. The DASH diet also emphasizes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and low-fat dairy while reducing saturated fat and added sugars.

## Important Note

This calculator provides guidance based on DASH guidelines. For personalized dietary advice, especially if you have hypertension or other health conditions, consult a registered dietitian or your physician.

faq.a: DASH stands for Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension. It's an NIH-backed eating plan designed to reduce blood pressure naturally.

faq.q: How much sodium per day on the DASH diet?
faq.a: Standard DASH recommends 2,300 mg of sodium daily. The stricter version limits it to 1,500 mg per day for maximum blood pressure reduction.

faq.q: What's the difference between salt and sodium?
faq.a: One gram of salt contains approximately 400 mg of sodium. This calculator converts between both measurements automatically.

faq.q: Where is hidden sodium in food?
faq.a: Bread, cheese, processed meats, canned vegetables, soups, and restaurant meals are major sources of hidden sodium.

faq.q: How much can DASH lower blood pressure?
faq.a: Studies show the DASH diet can reduce systolic blood pressure by 5-10 mmHg within 2 weeks when followed consistently.

faq.q: What are the main components of DASH?
faq.a: DASH emphasizes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and low-fat dairy while being low in saturated fat and added sugars.

faq.q: What are good examples of DASH-friendly foods?
faq.a: Berries, leafy greens, whole-grain bread, chicken breast, fish, beans, nuts, and low-fat yogurt are all excellent DASH choices.
Yes, all calculators on Hacé Cuentas are free and require no signup, registration, or payment of any kind.
